reviews.review_text: Could never get into racing games up until I tried this game. Pure adrenaline, pure excitement. I got towards the finish line, and off the cliff the next second. +10 seconds for a skill issue for me, but a whole +10 out of 10 points from me

This is THE rally game on steam, bar none. Sorry WRC. 

I recently played the original Colin McRae Rally, which codemasters also made, and its really cool and a lot of fun despite its age. Amazing they've been at it so long for rally titles, but this was probably their crowning achievement.

Who knew the WRC license, and with it, the expectations of extreme accuracy to the real life World Rally Championship, could be such a poison pill??
This game dives you into the rally world in a spectacular manner. It feels utterly realistic IF, of course, the settings are there to match whatever good driving wheel you might own.
super fun and realistic. good graphics and runs good on low end pc's
still best sim rally game imo { overall }  Loads of fun for hours and put ti down for year and pick it back up still the best lmao. not me the game

perfect auf jedenfall der goat of all rally games auch wenn campangne bisschen rené ist aber sonst nice. wennschon dann auch die goaty edition kaufen weil basegame hat original garnichts das find ich echt rené

Super cool game,its very realistic,has good ambient sound which makes you really feel like you are in that rally,typical banger made by Codemasters and in typical EA fashion the game requires you to buy a ton of dlcs if you want to have the full experience,only for them to abandon the game after a while only to release more WRC games
Love to play this game on the weekend, feels like you travel at beautifull and challanging rally track place

En definitiva el mejor juego realista sobre rally.
No apto para novatos, es un juego que te obliga a conducir bien y a toda velocidad lo quieras o no.
El dark souls de las carreras, no te perdona error.
As a rally driver, this is peak simulation, considering the year of release it's fantastic <3

Perfect mix of arcade and sim-ish gameplay. The full version is usually really cheap on sales and includes most of the content of the previous game. The physics are a bit off compared to realistic simulators (hence the "ish") and there's a few small details that can be nitpicked, but it's in general a very, very fun game and a really good buy.

I got this after buying a sim racing rig which I played Assetto Corsa, Beam NG, and Forza Horizon 5 on it first.  THIS IS THE BEST, NO CONTEST.  Single most exhilarating game I've EVER played.

PROS: YOU CAN USE H-PATTERN SHIFTERS WITH CLUTCH.  Graphics are good on a monitor, very good in VR.  Driving dynamics/physics are excellent and realistic- some say it's "just a bit arcade-y" but if you watch real rally runs and compare it really isn't.  The force feedback on my Moza R5 is superb.  Decent selection of courses and the variable surfaces are highly realistic.  Decent car selection, though I've fallen in love with the Lancia Delta HF Integrale and care little for the others now.  Great sense of speed, great sound.
CONS:  They're ending online club support now.  Wish there was more to the career complexity and car customization.

If you want a game that LITERALLY gets the adrenaline pumping, play this on a sim rig.  Even better with VR.  The better you get, the more intense it is... and you can get a LOT better.  I've been playing driving games my whole life and recently started sim racing with a rig, after 2 weeks I thought I had a great lap time of 8:23 just FLYING through on my favorite course (Time trial, Monte Carlo - Pra d'Alart using the Delta HF).  2 weeks later I got better at drift turns and was down to 7:54.  Current fastest lap is 7:41, and when you're doing that with force feedback in VR it is absolutely INSANE... and I still have a lot of room to improve.  If you made it this far, just buy the game.  Seriously.  Even with more modern titles out, this is still THE ONE.
Fun game, great content. Not so hardcore as others, still fun.
it's been 6 years and the game totally perfect physics for rally
Good game optimized with many challenges and works great on linux!

great game, onæy complain is the AI n the cross rally mode, they are f*cking brick and do NOT have any spacial awareness, they follow a line, and if your in the way they ram directly into you. Other than that it's great
Such a solid game. Physics are great, super optimized for all types of hardware, always has been fun playing this.

Very enjoyable on dirt where the feel is believable, except for the hand-brake which is way too "turn-friendly" at low speeds. 
On tarmac the game is less convinving. The transition between grip and slide shows the strange rubberbanding behavior known from earlier Codemaster titles which just doesn't feel right.

The safest start to overcome unforgiveness of drunk driving. Just take some (a lot) booze and get behind the wheel. This game will help a lot to master car control when unsober. 

It's a highly realistic sim, a lot of people argue RBR is more realistic, but according to most of those people nothing beats RBR physics and if one plays any other Rally game hell is guaranteed for them, not even driving a real car comes close to realism of RBR, how do you even dare to compare?!

To sum up, I think AC Rally will beat it in the future, but at this moment, no other Rally game has as much content as Dirt Rally 2.0. Got GOTY Edition on sale for less than 5 bucks and it was the wisest financial decision I've ever made. Recommended 100%

Colin McRae: Dirt 2 is hands down one of the best off-road racing games I’ve ever played. The graphics still look amazing, the soundtrack is perfect, and the racing feels fast, smooth, and exciting every time you play. The variety of tracks, vehicles, and events keeps the game fun for hours without getting repetitive. The career mode is super immersive, and the atmosphere makes you feel like you’re actually part of a real rally racing tour. Whether you’re drifting through dirt trails or flying over jumps, the gameplay is incredibly satisfying.

One thing I really appreciated is how much the game actually helped me understand driving fundamentals. It taught me things like controlling speed through corners, braking at the right time, maintaining traction, and reacting quickly to changing terrain. Even though it’s an arcade-style racing game, it surprisingly helped improve my coordination, reaction time, and overall confidence behind the wheel.
Fun and good, great performance too

Bought it on discount (it was 90% off, i think?). Best purchase I ever made.
I had fun crashing and losing on this game. Probably the only games I can enjoy learning from failure. And the right hand numbness when I 'passionately' tried to save my crash from spinning. Yes, I played on keyboard. It's worth it.
don't know where my play time has gone, but this a good game, way better than wrc 24, I'm sure I had about 40 hours, might be a different game, I dunno.
Tremendous fun, especially with a wheel. definitely worth picking up if you like racing games, I'd say that i prefer this over any other racing sim i've played so far

This game is definitely worth purchasing if you are like me and like to play career mode, or custom championships. There is a lot of discussion about the racenet servers being shut down in July of 2026. This will stop all online play, as well as acheivements, but it does not stop you from playing a career, and having full access to all cars and tracks.  
I will say it again, if you are an ONLINE ONLY player, then this is not for you.
You can play a career, which is very in depth in my opinion, and with the GOTY edition, you will get all of the tracks and cars, as well as historical scenarious and the Mcrae add on. There is SO much content 
here.
The graphics are great, the FFB is great,  each road feels right. When the dirt is dry, it can be rough and very bumpy. During or after rainfall, that dirt becomes mud, and you can feel this. Everything is sluggish and sloppy. It is brilliant. The weather is amazing. Driving on tarmac when you have small ice patches is just like real life. You run over them and will spin a bit, which can cause problems. You are constantly on the wheel in this game. Every turn, every bump, every corner, you are trying to hold on to a beast that is violently wanting to spin out one way or another. Just when you think you're doing great, you hit that little bump the wrong way, or you rear end just touches the edge which might have a rock or muddy edge.. then boom, you lose control.. maybe you can regain control, maybe not.
Damage is very good, having to repair and decide what repairs you do and which ones you have to skip. 
Broken a headlight but the next race is in the dark? Well you know your choices. Break both headlights while driving in the dark? Choose between dropping out and trying to make it back in the dark.  
Career mode (to me) is the most fun. You compete and really have to watch your budget. You don't win a ton of money, however, even finishing last, you get some type of payout. Not enough to buy a new car, but perhaps enough to fix your existing beast and maybe help train your engineer just a bit more. 
This simulator teaches patience and humility. It's not about winning every race. It's about surviving the race and figuring out how to get your car to the next event. Sooner or later you will be able to afford another vehicle. It's just a great experience. 

Recently those  of us who purchased GOTY edition received a full set of cars in the garage and 2.4  million credits. I've seen complaints about how this ruins career mode. There is an easy fix for this.
You sell all of the cars for 0$ each except for the Lancia (they won't let you sell it or the Opel from 2001).
Then you purchase an expensive car, and resell it (you'll take a loss of approximately 20%). I did this over and over until I had about 20K in the bank and one car for rallies. This allowed me to "start" a career with one car and a small amount of cash.  From here you can build  your career.

This game in my opinion is worth the 40 Euro for the GOTY edition. If on sale, it's a no brainer.

The definitive rally game. It also probably has the best WRX experience ever.
The standard rally gameplay is quiet forgiving and accessible for people who can't play with a precise inputs - I have played the game for hundreds of hours on keyboard.
The game's disadvantage is that its roster of vehicles is outdated and limited. Games like EA WRC has the most cars in that case. But then EA WRC don't have WRX. And DiRT does it best.
They do have the vehicles that are most adored by rally fans and famous stages.

The games graphics have aged incredibly well and are very well optimised. I play with a gtx 1050ti that has 4 gigs of vram plus 16 gigs of ddr4 ram. The only gripe I have is that the rain makes first person view basically impossible.

So if you want to play for the offline experience, it is perfect as a start before going to harder games.
But some achievements require online play and the servers are shutting down. It is unknown what the game's future looks like.
Great graphics, cars, and pretty realistic

I'd be playing more of this if I had one of those steering wheels with pedals for gaming.

VR Rally and challenging. I think its a great simulator and totally worth the buy if you want a white

The cars are easier to control than Dirt rally 1, but they punish you with car damage even if you're not crashing a lot. I prefer Dirt rally 1 where the challenge is controlling the car rather than artificial difficulty and also the cars just feel better.

I do generally like the idea of career mode having stuff like this, but this feels bad as a new player. My car barely moves after one race and then i don't even have the option of repair sometimes. In VR4 they subtracted money if you crashed. And i don't even mind damage persisting if it's implemented differently

I also dislike how in this and Dirt 1 your turn warnings disappear after a second whereas WR7 they persist until you get to the turn. I also dislike 10+ minute races where's dirt 1 was closer to 5 min. And 110 gb is way too much.
Still the best all around Rally experience on the market after 6 years.
I have an Intel UHD 620 onboard only i5 Laptop. This is a game that works very well on it. I'm happy.
it weas really good and i had a lot of fun watching my friend play it :}
